configurations.name: with_skill
configurations.description: Compete skill with CSO + Market Industry Analyst closed review loop

configurations.name: without_skill
configurations.description: Baseline — no skill guidance, no review loop

quality_notes: Both evals converged in 2 iterations — consistent review loop behavior
Analyst segment_relevance scored 5/5 on eval-27 — competitive sets are materially different across segments
Eval-26 (enterprise): Accenture, Capgemini, IBM, TCS, Arvato — global SI focus
Eval-27 (Stadtwerke): DATAGROUP, Bechtle, Computacenter, Arvato, SWK cooperatives — regional specialist focus
Review loop caught and fixed: bias in weaknesses, repetitive differentiation, one-dimensional trap questions, missing urgency deadlines
Baseline structural failures consistent: no source URLs, wrong field names, non-standard trap question schema

analyst_observations: Non-discriminating assertion: K-01 (3-5 competitors) passes in both configurations — not useful for differentiation
High-signal assertions: K-06 (source URLs) and K-02 (field names) discriminate perfectly — 100% with_skill, 0% without_skill
Review loop convergence is stable: both evals start ~3.4-3.6, converge to ~4.1 in exactly 2 iterations
Cost tradeoff: ~4x more tokens and ~4x more time for +40% pass rate and qualitative quality improvement (bias removal, positioning currency, stakeholder coverage)
Segment calibration works: analyst correctly validates different competitive sets for different markets
